first_paragraph: Ogdolo LLC, dba Boot.dev (“Boot.dev”), whose principal office is located at 3520 N. University Ave., Suite 300, Provo, UT 84604, owns and operates www.boot.dev (the “Site”), providing access to digital information and educational content related to learning backend development (our “Service”).
This Privacy Policy describes how your personal information is collected, used, and shared when you visit www.boot.dev (the “Site”).

ThePrimeagen spent 10 years at Netflix writing performance critical software. He has become deeply passionate about building developer tools and teaching others how to use them. He only codes in NeoVim, uses a split keyboard, and rebases his Git branches. And yes, he has a beautiful wife and children.

Lane is a back-end engineer and the lead instructor of Boot.dev. He has taught over one million students worldwide, on Boot.dev, FreeCodeCamp, YouTube and the Backend Banter podcast. Lane worked as a backend engineer writing Go, Python and TypeScript, then moved into engineering management and a couple of years later left to build Boot.dev full-time.

TJ DeVries (aka "teej") has never worked at Netflix, but if you've heard of Neovim, well, he's a maintainer! It's really not a big deal. He's also a top telescope.nvim contributor and knows how to read, which is impressive. He used to work at Sourcegraph before becoming a full time coding educator and content creator.

Sarah is an engineer and instructor at Boot.dev. She has been obsessed with making the computer do the thing since the late 2000s. Her career has been a complicated journey from embedded software and game development to mobile and web development. She strives to know a little bit about everything (and a lot about bash).
Ballan peered into the darkness of the temple from above, frowning.

Ballan wondered if it was courage or delusion that made him think he had a goblin's chance at chess of getting out of this situation alive. The odds were certainly stacked against him, propped up by his terrible spellcraft, bad luck, general demeanor, and of course, the hundred-or-so foot serpent that presently loomed over him.

"So - these crystals. Why are they important? What do they do?" Kahya asked as they stopped beside a creek for brunch. Two breakfasts are better than one, Boots was fond of saying. The bear controlled the pace of their travel, and was ultimately responsible for the destination. For much of the trip, Ballan and Kahya had been content to follow his lead.

Ballan frowned as he watched an airship with black flags careen away from Ruebenberg at high speed. That was not the leisurely pace of someone out for a pleasant skysail - he'd bet what little money he had left that someone had just stolen something. Kahya followed his gaze and frowned as well.

h1: Chapter 6: The Lord of the Mire
first_paragraph: Ballan had decided his wounded leg was worth an extended stay in civilization. A local innkeep had been kind enough to give the four of them room and board, and so, for the first time since his journey began, Ballan found himself in a proper bed. The room was well appointed, clean, with plenty of light. He glanced out the window... and was reminded that it had been wreathed in flowers. He sighed.

h1: Chapter 7: The Plan for the Prison
first_paragraph: Once they had returned from Auntie Nub's, they rested for a day or so before everyone piled into Ballan's room to discuss their plan. Boots had been surprisingly amenable to the idea of breaking someone out of prison, though Ballan still had his reservations. He had heard plenty of stories about the Wall…

When the morning of the attempted prison break arrived, Tilda appeared (smugly) with everything they needed to act as prison guards for Holgith. Boots left shortly after to locate the necessary juncture he would need to disable the anti-magic zone. The plan was to disable the zone around noon, about fifteen minutes after they arrived.

Boots set them down some distance away from the prison, in the shadow of the Western Ward. The enormous pylon, for all its purported magical properties, felt mostly inert to Ballan's senses. He supposed that was because the magic it was generating was diffused over a large area. Everyone knew the wards deterred larger monsters and other creatures from the Wilds from approaching the area. All to keep the city of Avony safe - and it worked. Avony was one of the safest places in the World, as far as Ballan knew.

Matt codes at Boot.dev. He was a student of Boot.dev who started here as an intern. Matt helps maintain the assignments by fixing typos and adding helpful tips and links to official docs. If you ever need help with an assignment, hop on the community Discord and @matt after making a help thread.

Dan is an admitted TypeScript and React enjoyer, NeoVim user (btw), and Junior Software Engineer at Boot.dev. He has an eclectic background in maths and performance coaching in squash. When he's not coding or maintaining courses, you can find him hanging out in the community Discord, probably still coding away.

Allan is a Software Engineer at Boot.dev. He has been programming for over 9 years and has a passion for teaching. He has his bachelors degree in computer science with an emphasis in Full-Stack Web Development. He enjoys spending time with his family, playing video games, and learning new technologies in his free time.

Hunter is an immortal DotA 2 player, a World of Warcraft (classic) enjoyer, and senior developer. He has been coding for over 7 years and loves to learn, build, and teach new technologies. He works on the Boot.dev game mechanics and helps write and maintain the courses.

Waseem is another Boot.dev student-turned-developer who is also a Neovim user (btw) and an Elixir enjoyer. He loves learning and tinkering with technologies, including the cloud for some reason. He is also a casual gamer and bass player.

first_paragraph: Isaac Flath was the Head of Data Science consulting at Centriam beforing making the switch to speaking and education in 2025. He's a data and AI specialist that has been using Python to build custom AI powered solutions for companies that want to understand their own information better.

Theo is a Persian and Arabic philologist with a PhD from the University of Chicago. He taught himself to code in Python and Rust for use in his research, and went on to work as a Research Software Engineer and MLOps Engineer on an NSF-funded project. Now he edits courses at Boot.dev, helping authors create highly engaging and effective learning experiences.
